title: 18 Steps to the Universe
author: Ros, Rosa M.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The Network for Astronomy School Education (NASE) is a program of the International Astronomical Union (IAU) within the Key Programs Working Group. NASE's main  mandate is to carry out activities and projects for teacher training, to promote the teaching of astronomy, and thereby foster future careers among university students. This program also raises awareness of astronomy in society, from the latest scientific developments to the cultural contents of our past. NASE's main objective is to develop quality training courses in all countries interested in promoting astronomy at different levels of education. It incorporates topics related to this discipline into various curricula and introduces young people to science through models and approaches to the study of the Universe. NASE began its journey at the IAU General Assembly in Rio de Janeiro in 2009. Initially, course materials were developed with astronomical and astrophysical content, and a book containing these core contents, entitled "14 Steps to the Universe," was published in 2014 and revised and supplemented in 2017. In recent years, NASE courses have incorporated astrobiology and cultural astronomy. Consequently, we prepared the book we are presenting, '18 Steps to the Universe', as a result. It is not only an update of the original NASE text, but also a new offering expanded in line with the latest scientific discoveries. The book includes 18 activities (conferences, workshops, and working groups) for the training of teachers interested in scientific content related to astronomy,  strophysics, astrobiology, and astroculture. They are the result of the work of a large number of professional astronomers and teachers who have participated as trainers in the courses over several years. These courses can also be found on the NASE website (www.naseprogram.org)
